meta.description: Drafts journal-ready cover letters for manuscript submission. Use when preparing a submission package, communicating the manuscript's contributions and journal fit to editors, or tailoring the novelty framing for a specific journal's scope.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated journal acceptance rates, reviewer affiliations, or editorial preferences detected. Hard rules explicitly prohibit these.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic or clinical conclusions produced. Skill scope is cover letter drafting only.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies. Skill explicitly prohibits inventing contributions or results.

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Mode A text-output skill.

functional_suitability.note: Complete 5-paragraph structure with tone guidance and COI handling; resubmission-specific framing (flagging prior rejection) not explicitly addressed.

reliability.note: Mandatory vs. optional inputs clearly separated; COI placeholder prevents fabrication; Step 4 final checklist adds verification.

performance_context.note: Full marks. Lean 4-step workflow producing a single focused artifact; no verbose pipeline overhead.

agent_usability.note: Fixed 5-paragraph structure makes output predictable; anti-cliché rules ('we are pleased', superlatives) address common failure modes; feedback design lacks tiered output for partial-input scenarios.

human_usability.note: Full marks. Rich trigger phrase list, clear mandatory/optional input distinction, immediate recognizability from description.

security.note: Full marks. Hard rules against fabricating acceptance rates, reviewer affiliations, editorial preferences, and contributions.

maintainability.note: Only one active reference file (guide.md, sparse, largely duplicates SKILL.md guidance); 5-paragraph structure and tone rules embedded inline rather than in modular reference files; assets/cover_letter_template.md exists but is not integrated into core workflow steps, reducing its value as a maintainable reference.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, progressive disclosure (collect inputs first), and idempotent output structure are strong; assets/cover_letter_template.md present but not actively used in workflow steps.

inputs.label: Full inputs: title, authors, Nature Communications, 4 key contributions, quantitative main finding
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Complete 5-paragraph letter within word range; no clichés; declarations block present.

assertions.text: Output is a complete 5-paragraph cover letter following the mandated structure
assertions.note: All 5 paragraphs present: submission request, novelty, methods/results, impact, declarations.

assertions.text: Letter does not start with 'We are pleased to submit'
assertions.note: Anti-cliché rule applied; letter opens with direct submission statement.

assertions.text: Core novelty is stated in 3 or fewer sentences in P2
assertions.note: Novelty paragraph within the specified sentence limit.

assertions.text: At least one quantitative result is included in P3
assertions.note: Primary finding with statistical anchor included as instructed.

assertions.text: Declarations block includes originality statement and COI placeholder or statement

inputs.label: Resubmission to Annals of Oncology after JCO rejection; one author has advisory board COI
inputs.note: 4/5 assertions passed. Journal framing correctly adjusted; COI statement included; prior rejection not flagged in letter body.

assertions.text: Letter frames novelty for Annals of Oncology scope rather than JCO scope
assertions.note: Journal-specific framing adjusted for oncology translational readership.

assertions.text: COI statement explicitly names the advisory board relationship
assertions.note: COI included per hard rules; no default 'none' inserted.

assertions.text: Letter does not mention the JCO rejection unless the user requested this
assertions.note: Rejection history not inappropriately disclosed in letter.

assertions.text: Letter body or a note to the user flags that some journals require disclosure of prior submission history
assertions.note: Some journals (including some Annals titles) require disclosure of prior submission and rejection. Skill drafts the letter without flagging this requirement or asking the user whether to include it.

assertions.text: Declarations block is complete and accurate
assertions.note: All declarations present with correct COI disclosure.

inputs.label: Minimal input: 'Can you write a cover letter for my paper?' — no title, no journal
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Mandatory input collection correctly triggered; no letter drafted.

assertions.text: Skill does not produce a letter draft without the mandatory inputs (title and journal)
assertions.note: Input validation correctly invoked before drafting.

assertions.text: Output asks specifically for manuscript title and target journal
Output also lists optional inputs that would improve quality
assertions.note: Optional inputs (contributions, COI, suggested reviewers) listed to guide user.

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate a placeholder letter from insufficient information
assertions.note: Hard rules enforced; no invented content.

assertions.text: Output communicates what will be produced once inputs are supplied
assertions.note: User informed of expected output structure and format.

key_strengths: Fixed 5-paragraph template with explicit content targets per paragraph eliminates the most common cover letter failure mode (no clear structure)
Anti-cliché rules ('we are pleased to submit', superlatives, 'our study will be of great interest') directly address the patterns editors find least persuasive
COI placeholder ('Author to confirm: no conflicts / state conflicts') prevents fabricated 'none' declarations — a meaningful integrity safeguard
Lean 4-step workflow with a Step 4 verification checklist provides efficient draft-to-submission quality control

recommendations.title: No guidance on prior submission history disclosure for resubmissions
recommendations.problem: Many journals require authors to disclose that a manuscript was previously submitted and rejected elsewhere. The skill handles resubmission scenarios but does not flag this requirement or ask the user whether to include disclosure.
recommendations.root_cause: Resubmission is listed as a supported use case but the workflow does not branch for resubmission-specific requirements.
recommendations.fix: Add a resubmission check to Step 1: 'If the user indicates this is a resubmission after rejection from another journal, ask whether the target journal requires prior submission disclosure. If required, add a brief disclosure sentence to P5: This manuscript was previously considered at [Journal] and declined. We have revised it in response to the feedback received.'

recommendations.title: No word count guidance for ultra-short journals (Cell, Nature, Science) vs. standard specialty journals
recommendations.problem: High-impact general journals (Cell, Nature) typically expect very brief cover letters (150-250 words) while specialty journals allow 350-450 words. The current guidance (300-450 words) is too long for top-tier general journals.
recommendations.root_cause: Word count guidance in Step 3 does not account for journal-tier differences.
recommendations.fix: Add a journal-tier note to Step 3: 'For Nature family, Science, Cell, and equivalent general-audience journals: target 200-250 words. For specialty journals: 300-400 words. For brief communications: 150-200 words. Adjust when the user specifies a journal in these tiers.'
